task: Write a Python function count_distinct_over(nums, k) returning how many distinct values in the list are strictly greater than k, each value counted once no matter how many times it repeats. An empty list returns 0. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The provided Python function correctly counts distinct values in the list greater than k.
trace: To accomplish this task, we first convert the list to a set which removes any duplicate values since sets only allow unique elements. Then, we iterate through each number in the set and use an if statement to check if it is strictly greater than k, incrementing our counter for each one that satisfies the condition. This solution uses set operations for O(n) time complexity for both removing duplicates and finding distinct numbers greater than k. Handling the edge case of an empty list by returning 0 was also addressed by initializing our counter with a conditional statement.
